text: I had the good fortune of working with Simon while I was at CFA.
Apart from being a pleasure to work with, he is one of the few people I've met with good graphic design skills as well as a natural flair for coding.

Analytical and thoughtful, he solves problems with innovative yet practical learning solutions. Simon's an experienced learning professional, a team player, has a calm persona and picks things up very quickly. I would be more than happy to work with Simon again and would happily recommend him to any company.
author: Kim Challis

text: Simon commenced his time at Northern Health as an eLearning Developer / Learning Designer, employed to support the analysis, design, development and implementation of online learning solutions and eLearning courses into our new Learning Management System.

In the time he worked under my supervision he was instrumental in supporting the team to implement and launch a new internal LMS and build an operational eLearning Service - a new direction for the business. It quickly became clear that he was a tremendous asset to our team through his strong commitment to making change and bringing fresh and contemporary ideas to the table.

Simon utilises sound instructional design skills coupled with strong graphic design, coding and web development skills that enable him to showcase innovative solutions, automate processes and take theoretical ideas and concepts and turn them into user friendly and engaging digital products. The end goal being to provide a unique end user experience. His passion for all things technology, continuous improvement, ongoing learning, and performance consulting will see him thrive in his future career. Simon was a pleasure to lead, a great team player and someone I would not hesitate to recommend.
author: Rebecca Gilbert

text: Simon has been a valued member of the team for some time and I have had the pleasure of supporting and watching his transition from web developer to digital learning design and development tasks.
He is a key project team member and contributes extremely well to projects, ensuring they stay on track and a quality learning program is developed.

I'm impressed by his passion for his own learning, self-motivation to continually improve and understanding of where the industry is heading. I'm still stunned Simon didn't move himself into instructional design earlier than he did! He has a skills set to offer that's unlike many others in that he understands graphic design, coding, web development, learning management and content management systems, and instructional design. He's an all-rounder for any design team!
author: Sarah Henshaw
